summary: Soundboard capture of a high‑energy New Haven show – standout Scarlet > Fire and The Other One, with solid but not flawless audio.
review: The second set from the April‑1983 New Haven run is repeatedly called one of the best, driven by a scorching Scarlet > Fire sequence and an epic The Other One that blows the roof off.  The SBD source is generally praised as clear and “right on,” though a few listeners note occasional pitch quirks and missing audience bass depth.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviewers give 4‑5 stars, highlighting exceptional performances (especially Scarlet > Fire and The Other One) while noting only minor recording imperfections.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear SBD mix with crisp mids; a few reports of fast‑pitched Scarlet/Fire, occasional channel “wavy gravy,” and reduced low‑end from missing audience bass, but overall listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑set momentum is relentless – high‑energy jams, tight transitions and a climactic run from Scarlet > Fire into The Other One that fans cite as the show’s apex.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his “groove pockets” and adventurous soloing throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly called a “monster” dropping bombs; one outlier mentions ear‑splitting volume, but overall his bass work is highlighted as a driving force.
